danger_signals.visual_form: Red-colored enemy ant colonies visible in overhead and side views
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat faction; direct combat engagement required
Environmental hazard (rain) has disrupted colony communication and navigation

danger_signals.visual_form: Spiders, caterpillars, antlions rendered visually larger than player ant
danger_signals.meaning: Existential threat requiring coordinated group response

danger_signals.visual_form: Destructive impact zones in yard view
Pesticide chemical zones in overhead view
danger_signals.meaning: Lethal environmental hazard; area becomes uninhabitable

danger_signals.visual_form: Destructive path cutting through yard zones
danger_signals.meaning: Seasonal environmental hazard eliminating surface territory

danger_signals.visual_form: Water accumulation at bottom of underground nests (side view)
danger_signals.meaning: Nest destruction and pheromone trail erasure

safety_signals.visual_form: Player-controlled ant highlighted in yellow color
safety_signals.meaning: Active player agency; indicates controllable entity with influence capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Grouped black ants following yellow ant or pheromone trails
safety_signals.meaning: Successful colony cohesion and following; organized force presence

safety_signals.visual_form: Visible scent-marked paths between food sources and nest
safety_signals.meaning: Functional communication network; resource flow established

safety_signals.visual_form: Visible food resources at nest location
Growing tunnel network in side-view cross-section
safety_signals.meaning: Colony growth and infrastructure development; stability increase

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Red ants serve as opposing faction in all game modes
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Black ant nest infrastructure; tunnels; food storage areas

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Red coloration for all enemy colony members
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Visually distinct from black ants; enemy status permanent
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Competing nest structures; separate tunnel networks; resource caches

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Not directly controlled; manifests through hazards (footsteps, pesticide, lawnmower)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: House structure; yard boundaries; electrical outlets; pest control applications

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Small black ant cluster in limited yard territory; underground nest barely developed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ants scatter searching for food; pheromone trails sparse; low organization

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Extended black ant presence across multiple yard zones; growing tunnel network visible in side-view
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Organized foraging lines; coordinated attacks on red ant positions; following yellow ant leadership

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Red and black ant clusters in proximity; visual overlap in contested zones
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat groups mobilized; ants converging on battle zones; pheromone trails directing reinforcements

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Severely reduced black ant population; fragmented nest structure; red ants dominating landscape
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Disorganized movement; lack of coordinated response; pheromone trails erratic

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Black ant colonies spread throughout yard and house; no red ant presence; multiple nest structures visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Calm foraging patterns; stable population; organized resource gathering; drones/queens visible mating

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Humans driven from house (Full Game victory)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Black ant colonies established inside house structure; full territorial control from yard to indoor spaces
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ants inhabiting human domestic spaces; no pesticide/hazard activity visible

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Water graphics in underground nest bottom; pheromone trails visible disappearing
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ant movement becomes erratic; coordination breaks down; navigation hazards increase

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Toxic zone overlay in affected yard regions; visual damage to colony infrastructure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Sudden ant mortality; panicked movement patterns; nest abandonment

environmental_storytelling.example: Overhead yard view shows surface territorial battles; simultaneous side-view cross-section reveals underground nest complexity and infrastructure depth—communicates ant civilization operating on multiple levels
House structure initially inaccessible; gradual ant infiltration shown through ants appearing in indoor zones; final state shows complete colony establishment indoors—narrates conquest narrative
Visible scent trails connecting nest to food sources communicate colony organization and efficiency; erased trails during rain show vulnerability to environmental forces
Ant clustering density indicates colony health and power; sparse populations communicate weakness or recent losses
Spiders, caterpillars, antlions rendered visibly larger than individual ants communicates threat level and necessitates group coordination
Growing tunnel networks in cross-section view show expanding civilization; compartmentalized storage and breeding chambers narrate social organization
Visible food piles in nest communicate successful economy and sustainability

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can be directly manipulated; leaves pheromone trails; carries food/objects; recruits followers; digs tunnels; initiates attacks
Move in organized lines toward marked destinations; prioritize scent-marked paths over direct navigation
Coordinate group attacks against red ants or predators; converge on combat zones; sacrifice individuals to overwhelm enemies
Scatter searching for food; ignore pheromone trails; movement erratic until food located
Defend nest perimeter; attack black ant intruders; expand territory; establish competing pheromone trails
Move predatorily; kill individual ants; require coordinated group response from black ants to defeat
Generate footstep impacts; apply pesticide; mow lawn; presence communicates defeat condition; removal is victory condition

notes: SimAnt communicates world-state primarily through visual colony organization and infrastructure visibility. The dual perspective (overhead + side-view) creates a cognitive model of ant civilization operating simultaneously on surface and underground levels. Faction identity (black vs. red) is absolute and color-coded; no diplomatic or alliance mechanics exist—only dominance/elimination. Danger signals manifest as environmental hazards (rain, pesticide, footsteps) and rival colony presence. The yellow player ant serves as agency indicator and leadership signal. Progression toward victory is visually narrated through geographical expansion (yard → house) and population density. The game's manifestation layer emphasizes emergent complexity from simple rules: individual ant behavior creates colony-level phenomena visible through pheromone trails, coordinated combat, and infrastructure growth.
